name: South Yorkshire (United Kingdom, de facto)
description: A blue field with a central white rose of York and other heraldic charges — an unofficial design representing South Yorkshire.
history: An unofficial flag for the South Yorkshire metropolitan county. South Yorkshire has no Flag Institute–registered county flag of its own; this design draws on the white rose tradition of the historic Yorkshire.

features.symbolism: an unofficial design featuring the white rose of York and other emblems for the metropolitan county
